I do not agree with the initial conclusion that the Judet view evaluates scaphoid fractures. While some may believe it assesses scaphoids, the actual Judet view is a radiographic technique used to examine the calcaneus — particularly for identifying avascular necrosis or fractures involving the posterior facet of the heel. In contrast, scaphoid evaluations rely more commonly on standard wrist views or specialized carpal projections such as the "scaphoid view" or "lateral wrist." Thus, despite common misconceptions, the true purpose of the Judet view lies in foot anatomy, not hand injuries.
